commit.message:
fix(release): Align pnpm setup and npm publish access

Remove the hardcoded pnpm v8 pin in release.yml so CI uses the repository packageManager version and avoids ERR_PNPM_BAD_PM_VERSION.

Set publishConfig.access=public for all scoped release packages so first-time publishes succeed as public scoped packages.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

build(deps): Bump workspace dependencies to latest

Upgrade root and junior package dependencies to current releases and refresh the lockfile.

Align the queued state adapter wrapper with the chat 4.17 StateAdapter contract by forwarding setIfNotExists.

Remove the obsolete @chat-adapter/slack patch since upstream subtype handling now allows file_share without local patching.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(deps): bump non-breaking Go module dependencies (#1064)

### Motivation
- Keep the repository's Go dependencies current with non-breaking
minor/patch upgrades per the CONTRIBUTING workflow by running `go get -t
-u ./...`.
- Avoid forcing a toolchain upgrade by pinning `github.com/cli/cli/v2`
to `v2.86.0` so the module remains compatible with the repo Go `1.25.2`
baseline.
- Refresh transitive modules and checksums to reduce unexpected
build/linker issues from stale `go.sum` entries.

### Description
- Bumped multiple direct dependencies in `go.mod`, including
`buf.build/go/protovalidate` → `v1.1.3`, `github.com/go-git/go-billy/v5`
→ `v5.8.0`, `github.com/go-git/go-git/v5` → `v5.17.0`,
`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` → `v0.44.1`,
`github.com/vektah/gqlparser/v2` → `v2.5.32`, the OpenTelemetry stack →
`v1.41.0`/`v0.66.0`, `golang.org/x/net` → `v0.51.0`, and
`google.golang.org/api` → `v0.269.0`.
- Refreshed many transitive module versions and checksums in `go.sum` to
reflect the updated module graph and added updated indirect entries for
modules such as `protoc-gen-validate`, `procfs`, and others.
- Explicitly retained `github.com/cli/cli/v2` at `v2.86.0` to avoid
requiring Go >= `1.25.7` in this repo's environment.

### Testing
- Ran `make install/dev` to install developer tooling, which completed
successfully.
- Ran `make lint` after tooling installation, which completed
successfully.
- Ran the test target (`export TZ=UTC; export TAGS="test_with_txtar";
make test`) and observed multiple test failures that appear to be
environment-dependent (version/tag assertions, missing external CLI
utilities used by some integration tests, and file-permission behavior
differences), so the full test suite did not pass in this environment.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69aa1c8c46f88324834ab28c70611f3b)

Signed-off-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): 新增 codex-session-reader skill (#30)

## Why
- 需要一个仅面向 Codex 的只读 session/thread 阅读 skill，便于在已知 thread id 时直接查看会话内容。
- 通过 `codex app-server` 官方接口读取数据，可以避免耦合底层本地存储格式，并降低后续维护成本。

## What
- 新增 `codex-session-reader` skill，提供 `read` 子命令，通过 `codex app-server`
读取单个 Codex thread。
- 支持 `--preview-only` 与 0-based、接近 Python 切片语法的 `--turns` 参数，用于控制输出范围。
- 更新根目录 `README.md` 的 skills 表格，补充 `codex-session-reader` 与遗漏的
`coderabbit-cli` 条目。

## Testing
- `uvx ruff check
skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py --help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
--help`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --preview-only`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns :5`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns -5:`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 10:-1`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 13`
- `./skills/codex-session-reader/scripts/codex_session_reader.py read
<thread-id> --turns 1:10:2`（确认报错）

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(sandbox): Add snapshot-backed runtime dependency bootstrapping (#74)

Add snapshot-backed sandbox bootstrapping for plugin-declared runtime
dependencies.

Plugins can now declare runtime dependencies in `plugin.yaml` via
`runtime-dependencies` (`npm` and `apt`). The runtime normalizes those
declarations into a deterministic whole-runtime profile and resolves a
matching sandbox snapshot before fresh session startup.

This change adds a full snapshot lifecycle for dependency profiles:
cache lookup, Redis locking, stale snapshot recovery, floating-version
max-age refresh, and manual rebuild epoch invalidation. Fresh sandboxes
now start from those snapshots when available.

Additional scope in this PR:
- GitHub plugin declares `gh` via apt and the issue helper now uses `gh
api`.
- Sentry plugin declares npm `sentry` (major-pinned selector) and skill
docs reference the preinstalled `sentry` CLI.
- Added tests for profile parsing, snapshot resolution/rebuild behavior,
and force-rebuild concurrency handling.
- Added canonical snapshot behavior documentation in
`specs/sandbox-snapshots-spec.md` and linked it from related specs/docs.

Fixes #61

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: GPT-5 <noreply@openai.com>

commit.message:
homey/pairing diagnostics: log driver pairing shape at init

Add bridge/node init-time diagnostics that log prototype methods and loaded pair view definitions from manifest. This verifies the runtime sees onPairListDevices and expected list/add templates when investigating list_devices spinner stalls.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey/pairing: remove custom onPair from list/add template flows

Root-cause fix: Homey list/add template flows now use SDK hooks only. Removed custom onPair methods from bridge/node drivers (which prevented list_devices dispatch in this flow), simplified pair manifests to list_devices -> add_devices, and updated harness/pairing-flow tests accordingly.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(examples): refresh onboarding and prune stale scripts

Summary:
- rewrite README around first-time EtherCAT bring-up and current
  public APIs
- fix docs and examples indices to point users at the maintained
  Livebooks first
- migrate the remaining useful low-level example scripts to the
  current Bus and EtherCAT APIs
- remove obsolete example scripts built around deleted Master and
  Bus APIs

Rationale:
- the repo still exposed broken example entrypoints that were worse
  than having fewer examples at all
- first-time users need an actionable path from bus discovery to
  process-data exchange without prior EtherCAT knowledge
- keeping only current examples lowers support load and makes the
  repo's harness surface more trustworthy

Validation:
- elixir -e 'for f <- ["examples/scan.exs", "examples/bench.exs",
  "examples/udp_test.exs", "examples/loopback.exs",
  "examples/sdo_debug.exs"] do
    Code.string_to_quoted!(File.read!(f), file: f)
  end'
- mix ethercat.harness.doctor
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

homey/pairing: use SDK onPairListDevices hook for list views

Stop registering list_devices as a session handler and rely on the SDK-native onPairListDevices hook (as in Homey templates). Keep custom handlers only for custom views/status endpoints. This removes the likely dispatch conflict where Homey never invoked list_devices.

- include profile source/rule match details in bridge pairing imported-node rows
- add warning rollups for reconnect churn and unresolved profile attribution
- expand presenter and harness tests for new diagnostics contract

- wait for node driver readiness in app diagnostics/snapshot APIs to avoid early Driver Not Initialized failures
- detach bridge-session client before stop and continue reconnect start even when stop fails
- coerce lock mode strings to boolean for inbound locked capability and support string targets for outbound when needed
- add tests for diagnostics readiness delays, stop-failure reconnect continuation, and lock coercion behavior

refactor(harness): add docs spine and repo doctor

Summary:
- add docs entrypoints, design and execution plan indices, a quality
  scorecard, and a harness-engineering design doc
- slim AGENTS.md into a routing layer and narrow usage_rules syncing to
  Elixir and OTP guidance only
- add `mix ethercat.harness.doctor` to mechanically validate the docs
  spine and AGENTS authored section size
- update README contributor guidance and add focused doctor tests

Rationale:
- the repo already had strong subsystem docs, but it lacked the
  structure and mechanical checks needed for an agent-first workflow
- AGENTS.md should act as a table of contents, while `docs/` remains the
  system of record for design history, plans, and quality tracking
- the doctor keeps this structure from drifting without turning the repo
  into documentation theater

Validation:
- mix ethercat.harness.doctor
- mix test test/ethercat/harness/doctor_test.exs
- mix test

repository.description: Experimental ham rig controller written in Rust - work in progress

commit.message:
fix(api): enforce signal direction and clarify staging

Summary:
- reject writing registered input signals and reading registered output
  signals in EtherCAT.Slave
- verify staged output bytes after `write_output/3` and document that the
  API confirms domain-buffer staging, not physical pin application
- rename master state fields to `slave_configs` and `domain_configs`
  and remove the unused slave-station Registry entry
- update slave/master architecture docs and add focused slave tests

Rationale:
- the public PDO API should enforce input/output direction explicitly
  instead of letting callers misuse registered signals
- generic PDO writes cannot honestly guarantee that hardware has already
  applied a value, so the API contract now matches what the runtime can
  really prove
- the master field rename and registry cleanup reduce ambiguity and keep
  internal naming aligned with the actual data shape

Validation:
- mix test

commit.message:
feat(dc): align distributed clocks runtime and API

Summary:
- add a spec-focused DC review, public API proposal, and execution plan
- replace the old DC surface with `EtherCAT.DC.Config`, `DC.Status`,
  `dc_status/0`, `reference_clock/0`, and `await_dc_locked/1`
- keep DC as a separate runtime process with its own FRMW-based
  maintenance and lock diagnostics instead of coupling it to domains
- move slave sync config and CoE helpers under `EtherCAT.Slave.Sync.*`
  and expose named sync/latch intent through slave config
- add a hardware validation Livebook and update examples, docs, and
  tests for the new runtime shape

Rationale:
- DC is a network-wide clock subsystem, not a domain concern, so the
  runtime owner should stay separate from process-image domains
- the public API now distinguishes clearly between master-wide DC
  infrastructure and per-slave sync behavior
- the new docs and notebook make the current behavior easier to verify
  on real hardware and easier to compare against the EtherCAT spec

Validation:
- mix test
- parsed all Elixir blocks in
  examples/hardware_validation_livebook.livemd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
